visa_free.notes: Holders of passports issued by governments of countries belonging to the European Union are exempt from visa requirements per the Visa Requirements page (category 10 of exemptions)

visa_policy.notes: Visa policy sourced from the official Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office PDF (dated 22 June 2022) and the live Visa Requirements page (passport.govmu.org, verified June 2026 and re-confirmed June 2026-29). The main exemptions page lists: citizens of Mauritius; residents of Mauritius; spouse/child of citizens/residents; vessel crew members on duty or transiting; holders of UN/COMESA/SADC/other international organization laissez-passer; INTERPOL passport holders on official mission; holders of laissez-passer from African Reinsurance Corporation and African Development Bank Group travelling on official mission; EU citizen passport holders; transiting passengers remaining only during vessel stay. Countries requiring visa prior to travel (not on arrival): Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Iraq, Laos, Libya, Mali, North Korea, Pakistan, Palestinian State, Saharawi Republic, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Yemen. Guyana: specifically noted on the Visa Requirements page as requiring visa prior to travel. Tourist visa allows up to 6 months per calendar year. Visa is issued free of charge. PRE-ARRIVAL DIGITAL FORMS (CONFIRMED JUNE 2026 ON OFFICIAL MAURITIUS SOURCE): All foreign nationals must complete BOTH the Mauritius All-in-One Travel Form AND the Health Declaration Form at the point of departure, within 72 hours prior to arrival; these are universal health/immigration pre-arrival declarations, not a visa or ETA - they do not change which nationalities may enter or on what terms. Source: official Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office Travel Advisory dated 12 June 2026 (https://passport.govmu.org/passport/?p=1448). ENTRY BAN (EFFECTIVE 6 JUNE 2026): Mauritius has instituted an entry ban on foreign nationals who have traveled from, transited through, or been in the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda, or South Sudan within 21 days prior to arrival (regardless of nationality). Mauritius citizens and permanent residents are admitted but subject to a 21-day quarantine. This is a travel-history-based public-health restriction, not a nationality-based visa policy change. Source: official Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office Travel Advisory 12 June 2026 (https://passport.govmu.org/passport/?p=1448). ETA SYSTEM (NOT YET LAUNCHED): An Electronic Travel Authorization system was announced by the Prime Minister in December 2025 and referenced again in the 2026-2027 Budget Speech (which describes an 'e-Visa/ETA' amending the Immigration Act to require all non-citizens to apply online and pay a prescribed fee prior to travel). Launch is expected during 2027 after technical development; scope, applicable nationalities, fee amount, and whether it replaces or supplements the current visa-free/VoA system are not yet defined on official Mauritius government pages as of June 2026-29.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for stays up to 90 days. Exemption does NOT apply to passport holders from: Afghanistan, Iraq, Kosovo, Libya, Palestine State, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Yemen.

Bilateral Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the Republic of Cote d'Ivoire on reciprocal exemption of entry visas for holders of diplomatic and official passports. Nationals of both countries holding valid diplomatic or service passports are exempted from visa entry requirements for stays of less than 90 days. Applicable to persons assigned to a Diplomatic Mission, Consular Post, Permanent Mission and Organisation in the other State or attending meetings and conferences. Cabinet approved signing on 28 March 2025; formal entry into force date not yet confirmed on official Mauritius immigration page.

cbi.program_name: Naturalization for Investors (Section 9(3) Mauritius Citizenship Act)

options.notes: Under section 9(3) of the Mauritius Citizenship Act, an investor may be naturalized as a citizen if: (i) he has invested a sum of not less than USD 500,000 in Mauritius; and (ii) he has resided in Mauritius for a continuous period of not less than 2 years preceding the date of application. This is naturalization-by-investment (investor route), not a standalone CBI program with a direct donation option. Application submitted to the Secretary for Home Affairs; declaration made before a Supreme Court Judge or Magistrate; applicants must place advertisements in two daily newspapers.

cbi.notes: Mauritius does not have a dedicated citizenship-by-investment program (no donation-only CBI). The only investment pathway to citizenship is via naturalization under Section 9(3) of the Mauritius Citizenship Act, which requires both a minimum investment of USD 500,000 AND 2 years of continuous residency. Dual citizenship policy not explicitly stated on the official DHA citizenship page. Contact: pmo.citizenship@govmu.org.

programs.name: Occupation Permit – Investor Category (Option 1)
programs.notes: Minimum investment of USD 50,000. Occupation Permit valid for 10 years, renewable. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after at least 5 years as Investor OP holder with cumulative turnover of MUR 20 million over 5 years (MUR 1.5 million in year 1). Year 6+ renewal requires MUR 5 million annual turnover. Application fee USD 1,000 upon approval; USD 50 non-refundable application fee. Updated August 2025.

programs.name: Occupation Permit – Investor Category (Option 2)
programs.notes: Minimum investment of USD 100,000. Occupation Permit valid for 10 years, renewable. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after at least 5 years with cumulative turnover of MUR 15 million over 5 years (MUR 1 million in year 1). Year 6+ renewal requires MUR 5 million annual turnover. Higher investment threshold with lower turnover target compared to Option 1. Updated August 2025.

programs.name: Permanent Residence Permit (PRP) – Real Estate Investment
programs.notes: An investor who invests at least USD 375,000 in a qualifying business activity is eligible to apply for the 20-year Permanent Residence Permit. Foreign nationals may also acquire residential property under IRS, RES, PDS or Smart City Scheme (SCS) to obtain residency. 20-year PRP is renewable. Exact minimum purchase prices for IRS/RES/PDS/SCS schemes require verification on EDB/official property scheme pages.

programs.name: Retired Non-Citizen Residence Permit
programs.notes: Available to foreign nationals above 50 years of age. Occupation Permit (Retired) issued for up to 5 years (changed from 10 years effective August 2025). Requires transfer of at least USD 2,000 per month (USD 24,000 per year) to a Mauritian bank account and minimum 180 days per year residency in Mauritius. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after holding the Residence Permit for at least 5 years with total transfer of at least USD 200,000 over the 5-year period. Application fee USD 50 (non-refundable). CORRECTION: Prior record stated 10-year permit duration and USD 54,000 over 3 years; updated to reflect August 2025 rule changes.

programs.name: Occupation Permit – Professional Category
programs.notes: Allows foreign professionals employed by a company in Mauritius to work and reside. Minimum salary: MUR 30,000/month. Valid for up to 3 years or duration of employment contract. Eligible for 20-year PRP after at least 5 years with a basic monthly salary of at least MUR 400,000 for 5 consecutive years. Expert Pass tier available at MUR 250,000/month. CORRECTION: Prior record stated MUR 150,000/month for PRP and 3-year path to PR; updated per August 2025 rule changes.

programs.name: Expert Pass
programs.notes: New category introduced effective August 2025 for highly skilled professionals earning at least MUR 250,000/month. Administered through NELS (National Electronic Licensing System). Full eligibility criteria and PRP pathway not yet confirmed on official Passport Immigration Office page.

programs.name: Occupation Permit – Self-Employed Category
programs.notes: Effective August 2025: minimum investment of USD 50,000 in services sector only. Must provide letters of intent from at least three clients (two local). Annual income targets: MUR 750,000 in year 1, scaling to MUR 6 million cumulative over 5 years. Occupation Permit issued for up to 10 years. Eligible for 20-year PRP after at least 5 years with annual business income of at least MUR 3 million for 5 consecutive years. Application online via NELS. CORRECTION: Prior record did not specify services-sector restriction or updated turnover targets.

programs.name: Young Professional Occupation Permit (YPOP)
programs.notes: For foreign students who have completed at least an undergraduate degree at a Mauritius institution recognized by the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC). Application must be submitted not later than 6 months after publication of results. Business activity must fall within: Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Fintech, Robotics, Financial Services, Information Technology. Effective August 2025: valid for maximum 2 years (reduced from 3 years), minimum salary MUR 25,000/month. After 2 years, must transition to standard Occupation Permit. Established under Section 13 of the Economic Development Board Act 2017 and Section 9A of the Immigration Act 1970. CORRECTION: Prior record stated 3 years; updated per August 2025 rule changes.

programs.name: Family Occupation Permit
programs.notes: Established under section 14 of the Immigration Act 2022 and section 13(1)(b) of the Economic Development Board Act 2017. Allows an applicant, spouse, dependent child, parent, other dependent, or person working exclusively for the family unit to become a resident for 10 years.

programs.name: Premium Visa (Premium Travel Visa)
programs.eligibility: Nationals from 180+ countries including the US, UK, Canada, Australia, France, Germany, Japan, Singapore, UAE and most European nations. Applicant must not enter the Mauritius labour market; main place of business and source of income must be outside Mauritius; must have proof of long stay plans and sufficient travel and health insurance.
programs.processing_time: Online application; visa issued by email after review
programs.notes: Valid for a period exceeding 6 months up to 1 year, renewable. For stays over 180 days in a calendar year. Free of charge (no processing fee). Citizens from countries not on the eligible list may apply online during a tourist visa stay. Administered by EDB Mauritius. No specific minimum income requirement stated. CORRECTION: Prior record stated 'approximately 130 countries'; updated to 180+ per official EDB page verified June 2026.

agreements: COMESA (Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa) – Mauritius is a member; holders of COMESA laissez-passer are exempt from Mauritius visa requirements
SADC (Southern African Development Community) – Mauritius is a member; holders of SADC laissez-passer are exempt from Mauritius visa requirements

validation_notes: CORRECTED (June 2026): Retired Non-Citizen Residence Permit duration changed from 10 years to 5 years, per August 2025 immigration rule changes. Minimum transfer requirement updated from USD 54,000 over 3 years to USD 24,000/year (USD 2,000/month), with 180 days/year residency required. PRP eligibility path extended from 3 years to 5 years, with total transfer of USD 200,000 over 5 years.

CORRECTED (June 2026): Occupation Permit – Professional Category: minimum salary threshold updated to MUR 30,000/month; PRP eligibility path extended from 3 to 5 years; PRP salary threshold updated from MUR 150,000/month to MUR 400,000/month, per August 2025 changes.

CORRECTED (June 2026): Occupation Permit – Investor Category: now two options (USD 50,000 and USD 100,000) with differentiated turnover targets (MUR 20M vs MUR 15M cumulative over 5 years); PRP eligibility now 5 years (not unspecified).
CORRECTED (June 2026): Self-Employed OP: now restricted to services sector, USD 50,000 minimum investment, updated turnover targets (MUR 750K year 1, MUR 6M cumulative over 5 years).
CORRECTED (June 2026): Young Professional OP: validity reduced from 3 years to 2 years, minimum salary MUR 25,000/month per August 2025 changes.
ADDED (June 2026): Expert Pass - new high-skill tier at MUR 250,000/month introduced August 2025.
CORRECTED (June 2026): Premium Visa eligible countries updated from 'approximately 130' to '180+' per official EDB residency.mu page.
ADDED (June 2026): ETA announcement noted - Prime Minister announced ETA system in December 2025; not yet launched as of June 2026.

CONFIRMED (June 2026): Visa_free and visa_on_arrival country lists and structure match the 2022 official PDF and live official page as of June 2026. No new nationality-level changes to visa_free/VoA found on official sources. The 2022 PDF remains the latest country-wise official list on passport.govmu.org as of June 2026.

CORRECTED (June 2026-29): Pre-arrival form requirement updated - the official Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office Travel Advisory (12 June 2026, https://passport.govmu.org/passport/?p=1448) specifies that travelers must complete BOTH the All-in-One Travel Form AND the Health Declaration Form at the point of departure (not just the All-in-One Travel Digital Form as previously noted from US State Dept source). Neither form is a visa or ETA classification change.

CONFIRMED (June 2026-29): Entry ban effective 6 June 2026 - now confirmed against the official Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office Travel Advisory (https://passport.govmu.org/passport/?p=1448). Foreign nationals who have been in DRC, Uganda, or South Sudan within 21 days of arrival are denied entry; Mauritius citizens/PR face 21-day quarantine. Travel-history public-health restriction only.

CONFIRMED (June 2026-29): Visa_free and visa_on_arrival country lists re-verified against live passport.govmu.org/?page_id=605 and Travel Requirements pages. No new nationality-level visa policy changes found. The 2022 PDF remains the latest country-wise official list as of June 2026-29.

UPDATED (June 2026-27): ETA/e-visa note updated - the 2026-2027 Budget Speech includes a formal proposal to amend the Immigration Act to introduce a digital ETA/e-visa for all non-citizens with a prescribed fee, expected 2027. System is not yet operational; current visa-free and VoA classifications remain valid.
CONFIRMED (June 2026): CBI - USD 500,000 + 2 years residency requirement confirmed against live dha.govmu.org page.
CONFIRMED (June 2026): Conditional_access diplomatic/official passport exemption (90 days, exclusion list) confirmed against live passport.govmu.org page.
gaps: The visa requirements PDF from passport.govmu.org is dated 22 June 2022; a more recent version may exist on the official site. The max_stay_days for 'No visa required' entries are not stated explicitly in the official PDF. Tourist visa duration is up to 6 months per calendar year per other official pages, but specific per-nationality stay limits for visa-free entries are not enumerated in the official country-wise list.
Guyana is specifically noted on the Visa Requirements page as requiring a visa prior to travel (not listed in the country-wise PDF table), so it is excluded from visa_free and visa_on_arrival.

An ETA/e-visa system was announced by Prime Minister Ramgoolam in December 2025 and again in the 2026-2027 Budget Speech (confirmed June 2026 from news sources; official budget annex confirms Immigration Act amendment planned). Launch expected 2027. Scope (whether it replaces visa-free access or supplements it), applicable nationalities, fee amount, and validity period remain undefined on official Mauritius government pages as of June 2026.

The Mauritius pre-arrival form requirements (All-in-One Travel Form AND Health Declaration Form) have been confirmed on the official Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office Travel Advisory dated 12 June 2026 (https://passport.govmu.org/passport/?p=1448). The Travel Requirements page (page_id=587) also references the All-in-One Travel Digital Form at safemauritius.govmu.org. These forms do not constitute a visa classification change. The official safemauritius.govmu.org page was not directly re-fetched in this validation pass.

Réunion Island passport-free scheme: French nationals resident of Réunion Island may enter Mauritius on a valid French identity card (instead of passport) for holiday stays of at least 2 nights. This was originally authorized from 19 September 2022 to 30 June 2023 (official passport.govmu.org announcement). Third-party sources report extensions of this scheme into 2024 and 2026, but the official Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office page has not been updated to reflect extensions beyond June 2023. Current validity of this scheme is unconfirmed on official sources as of June 2026.
No standalone donation-based CBI program found on official sources; only the naturalization-by-investment pathway under Section 9(3) of the Citizenship Act.
Dual citizenship policy for investor naturalization applicants not explicitly stated on the official DHA citizenship page.
Processing time for investor naturalization (CBI pathway) not stated on official page.
Exact minimum purchase prices for IRS/RES/PDS/SCS real estate schemes not stated on the official Passport Immigration Office page; EDB/official property scheme pages required for exact figures.

No official Mauritius government source found for credential-based conditional entry rules (i.e., no rule granting visa-free/VoA/e-visa entry based on holding a valid US, UK, Schengen, Canada, Australia, GCC, or other foreign visa or residence permit).
No OCI or equivalent special-status cardholder entry privilege found on official Mauritius immigration sources.

Wikipedia reports that Mainland China diplomatic/official/service passport holders receive a 60-day (not 90-day) exemption; this could not be verified on an official Mauritius government page and is therefore not recorded in conditional_access.

Cote d'Ivoire bilateral diplomatic/official passport agreement (conditional_access entry 2): Cabinet approved signing on 28 March 2025 (pmo.govmu.org official) but formal entry-into-force date not confirmed on the Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office website.

A Ukraine–Mauritius general visa waiver agreement was reportedly signed on 8 August 2024 (per Interfax Ukraine). Ukraine is already listed as visa-free for ordinary passports. Formal confirmation on official Mauritius government page not found.
Expert Pass (MUR 250,000/month) full eligibility criteria and PRP pathway not yet published on official Passport Immigration Office page; sourced from third-party immigration advisory (ibn.co.za).

August 2025 RBI changes (retirement permit, professional OP, YPOP, investor OP options, self-employed) sourced from immigration advisory (ibn.co.za/visaverge.com); exact implementing regulations on official govmu.org or EDB Mauritius pages not directly fetched - should be verified against official NELS/EDB portal when available.

outbound_official.notes: Source is the official Mauritius Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade PDF titled 'VISA REQUIREMENTS FOR MAURITIAN NATIONALS TRAVELLING ABROAD', updated 17 November 2025, published at foreign.govmu.org. The document covers 203 destinations. Visa-free entries reflect countries listed as 'Visa not required' or equivalent. Visa-on-arrival entries reflect countries listed as 'Visa on arrival'. E-visa entries reflect countries requiring an electronic visa obtained prior to or upon departure via an online portal (Cambodia e-visa, Sri Lanka ETA, Togo e-visa, Turkey e-visa). Serbia and UK require an ETA. UK also allows visa-free entry up to 6 months but requires an ETA for short-term visits if no valid visa is held. Rodrigues Island is an autonomous island within Mauritius; it is listed in the document as 'Visa not required'. Countries requiring visa prior to departure (not included in the above categories) include: Afghanistan, Algeria, Argentina, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, Belarus, Bhutan, Bolivia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Brazil, Brunei Darussalam, Burkina Faso, Canada, Cape Verde, Central African Republic, China (Taiwan), Colombia, Cuba, Democratic Republic of Congo, Dominican Republic, Egypt, El Salvador, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Guatemala, Guinea, Guinea Bissau, Guyana, Haiti, Honduras, India, Iraq, Ireland, Kazakhstan, Korea (North), Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Lebanon, Liberia, Libya, Macedonia, Mali, Marshall Islands, Mauritania, Mexico, Micronesia, Moldova, Monaco, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, Myanmar, New Zealand, Niger, Oman, Pakistan, Papua New Guinea, Paraguay, Peru, Sao Tome and Principe, Saudi Arabia, Sierra Leone, Solomon Islands, Sudan, South Sudan, Suriname, Syria, Tajikistan, Tanzania (visa-free but note: document says 'Visa not required' with exemption from visa fees for SADC nationals), Tchad, Timor Leste, Tonga, Turkmenistan, Ukraine, Uruguay, USA, Uzbekistan, Vanuatu, Vatican, Venezuela, Vietnam, Yemen.

visa_types.name: Standard Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-term leisure visits to Mauritius for tourism purposes.
visa_types.notes: Maximum stay of 6 months per calendar year. Visa is free of cost. Processing is 12 working days; applicants should apply at least one month before travel. Applied for through Mauritius diplomatic/consulate representatives. A visa does not guarantee admission - the final decision rests with the Immigration Officer.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visits to establish or conduct business or investment activities in Mauritius. Applicant must not receive any payment within Mauritius.
visa_types.notes: Maximum 120 days per calendar year; individual trip stays capped at 90 days. A Multiple Entry Business Visa exists for repeated business visits under the same 120-day annual cap. Requires a letter from a local sponsor or company. Applicant must not receive payment within Mauritius. Free of charge.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Passage through Mauritius to a third country without leaving the transit area, valid for within a 24-hour period.
visa_types.notes: Valid for a 24-hour transit period only. Free of charge.

visa_types.name: Medical Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals seeking medical treatment at registered private healthcare institutions in Mauritius.
visa_types.notes: Duration is for the treatment period, not exceeding 6 months. Treatment must occur at facilities registered with the Ministry of Health. Free of charge.

visa_types.name: Social Visa
visa_types.purpose: For socio-cultural, community, or religious activities in Mauritius, sponsored by recognized socio-cultural or religious organizations.
visa_types.notes: Maximum stay of 45 days. Requires a letter from the sponsoring socio-cultural or religious institution in Mauritius. Free of charge.

visa_types.name: Wedding Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows a foreign national to enter Mauritius for the purpose of civil marriage to a Mauritian citizen.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must be a national of a country listed in the 9th, 10th, or 11th schedules of the Passport Regulations. Requires proof of financial capacity (USD 50 per day), confirmed letter from Mauritian fiancé/fiancée, character/criminal record certificate, full birth certificate, and return ticket. Mauritian partner must show minimum monthly income of MUR 45,000. An extension of the Wedding Visa is also available (page_id=635). Free of charge.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Permits non-citizens to pursue full-time or part-time education at recognized tertiary institutions or TVET centers in Mauritius.
visa_types.notes: Eligible for certificate/diploma (minimum 1 academic year), undergraduate, postgraduate, postdoctoral, and exchange program students (up to 1 year). Application must be submitted by the educational institution, not the student directly. Requires letter of admission, proof of financial capacity, accommodation proof, and medical certificate (including HIV/Hepatitis B tests and chest X-ray). Tourists cannot convert their status to student. Duration varies by program. Renewal requires attendance records and continued enrollment proof. Free of charge.

visa_types.name: Workshop, Events and Conference Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals attending workshops, events, or conferences hosted by recognized institutions or ministries in Mauritius.
visa_types.notes: Requires a covering letter from the relevant Mauritius Ministry or Institution confirming the event. Free of charge.

visa_types.name: Premium Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals including tourists, retirees, and remote workers to relocate to Mauritius for an extended stay of up to one year, working or conducting business remotely from outside Mauritius.
visa_types.notes: Valid for stays exceeding 6 months up to 1 year, renewable. Targeted at remote professionals, retirees, and long-stay tourists. Applicant's main place of business and source of income must be outside Mauritius; must not enter the Mauritius labour market. Requires proof of travel and health insurance. Application is through the Economic Development Board (EDB) online portal at residency.mu. Available to nationals of 180+ eligible countries. Free of charge. Administered by EDB Mauritius.

visa_types.name: Retired Non-Citizen Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Long-stay residence permit for foreign nationals aged 50 and above who wish to retire in Mauritius.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 5 years (effective August 2025; previously 10 years). Requires transfer of at least USD 2,000 per month (USD 24,000 per year) to a Mauritian bank account and minimum 180 days per year residency. Non-refundable application fee of USD 50. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after 5 years with total transfers of at least USD 200,000 over the 5-year period. Applied for through the NELS (National Electronic Licensing System) portal. Must be aged 50+.

visa_types.name: Occupation Permit – Professional Category
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign professionals employed by a company in Mauritius to work and reside in the country.
visa_types.notes: Minimum salary MUR 30,000/month. Valid for up to 3 years or duration of employment contract. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after at least 5 years with a basic monthly salary of at least MUR 400,000 for 5 consecutive years. Applied for via NELS. USD 50 non-refundable application fee.

visa_types.name: Expert Pass
visa_types.purpose: A residence and work permit for highly skilled foreign professionals earning above a senior salary threshold, introduced August 2025.
visa_types.notes: New category effective August 2025. Minimum salary of MUR 250,000/month. Administered through NELS. Full eligibility criteria and PRP pathway details not yet fully published on official pages as of June 2026.

visa_types.name: Occupation Permit – Investor Category
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign investors who invest in a business in Mauritius to reside and manage that investment.
visa_types.notes: Two investment options: Option 1 requires minimum USD 50,000 with cumulative turnover target of MUR 20 million over 5 years; Option 2 requires USD 100,000 with MUR 15 million cumulative over 5 years. Occupation Permit valid for 10 years, renewable. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after 5 years. Application fee USD 1,000 upon approval; USD 50 non-refundable application fee. Applied for through NELS.

visa_types.name: Occupation Permit – Self-Employed Category
visa_types.purpose: Allows self-employed foreign entrepreneurs in the services sector to reside and operate their business in Mauritius.
visa_types.notes: Restricted to the services sector (effective August 2025). Minimum investment of USD 50,000. Must provide letters of intent from at least 3 clients (2 local). Annual income target: MUR 750,000 in year 1, scaling to MUR 6 million cumulative over 5 years. Valid for up to 10 years. Eligible for 20-year PRP after 5 years with annual income of at least MUR 3 million per year. Applied for via NELS.

visa_types.name: Young Professional Occupation Permit (YPOP)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign graduates of recognized Mauritius tertiary institutions to work in designated high-tech sectors after completing their studies.
visa_types.notes: Available to foreign students who completed at least an undergraduate degree at a TEC-recognized Mauritius institution. Must apply within 6 months of publication of results. Valid for maximum 2 years (reduced from 3 years effective August 2025). Minimum salary MUR 25,000/month. Permitted sectors: Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Fintech, Robotics, Financial Services, Information Technology. Must transition to a standard Occupation Permit after 2 years. Established under Section 13 of the Economic Development Board Act 2017 and Section 9A of the Immigration Act 1970.

visa_types.name: Family Occupation Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows a dependent family member or person employed exclusively for the family unit of an Occupation Permit holder to reside in Mauritius.
visa_types.notes: Established under section 14 of the Immigration Act 2022 and section 13(1)(b) of the Economic Development Board Act 2017. Covers spouse, dependent children, parent, other dependents, or a person working exclusively for the family unit. Valid for 10 years.
